title: Module 3 knowledge check
markdown: Why deep learning took off, and what it can and cannot do.

questions.prompt: In plain language, "deep" in deep learning refers to:
choices: Many layers in a neural network
Always using the deepest ocean data

questions.prompt: Which trio best explains why modern deep learning became practical?
choices: Large datasets, more compute, and better methods/architectures

questions.prompt: A fluent neural-network output is guaranteed to be factually correct.
Deep learning is often comparatively strong at:
choices: Pattern recognition in images, audio, or text when relevant data is plentiful
Proving mathematical theorems with zero error by default

questions.prompt: A common risk of deep models trained on skewed data is:
choices: Amplifying bias present in the training data

questions.prompt: Most learners and professionals using chatbots are typically:
choices: Running inference on models trained by others
Training a new foundation model from scratch each prompt
Performing unsupervised clustering of their keyboard
